message:
feat(core): add err.h message formatter (bd-err-1)

First sub-bead of bd-err-epic (port err.h to native Rust). Lifts the
message-shape logic from write_err_message in
frankenlibc-abi/src/err_abi.rs into a pure-safe core module so it's
independently unit-testable.

frankenlibc-core/src/err/mod.rs:
- pub fn format_err_message(progname, message, errno_msg) -> Vec<u8>
  Canonical BSD-style "progname: [message[: errno_msg]]\n" line.
  Handles all 4 combinations of empty/non-empty message + Some/None
  errno_msg without producing double-separator artifacts.
- pub fn basename_bytes(s) -> &[u8] for argv[0] normalization.

8 unit tests pass:
- basename_strips_dirs (5 cases incl empty, root-only, no-slash)
- warnx_message_only, warn_message_with_errno
- warn_empty_message_with_errno_no_double_separator
- warnx_empty_message_no_errno
- always_ends_with_newline (9-combo property test)
- empty_progname_still_produces_separator
- binary_safe_message_bytes (embedded NUL preserved)

frankenlibc-abi rebuilds cleanly against the new core module.

Closes bd-err-1; bd-err-2 will wire the abi shims.
